title: 春晓
author: 孟浩然
content:
春眠不觉晓
处处闻啼鸟
夜来风雨声
花落知多少
translation:
In spring sleep, unaware of dawn
Everywhere I hear birdsong
Last night came sounds of wind and rain
How many flowers have fallen?
annotation: A gentle morning poem about the fleeting beauty of spring. Meng Haoran captures that half-awake moment when nature's sounds drift into your dreams. The final question lingers — we never get the answer, and that's the point.

title: 静夜思
author: 李白
content:
床前明月光
疑是地上霜
举头望明月
低头思故乡
translation:
Before my bed, bright moonlight
I wonder if it's frost on the ground
Lifting my head, I gaze at the bright moon
Lowering it, I think of my hometown
annotation: Li Bai wrote this homesick masterpiece in understated simplicity. The moon has been a symbol of reunion in Chinese culture for millennia — everyone under the same moon, yet separated by mountains and rivers. Sometimes the simplest poems hit the hardest.

title: 登鹳雀楼
author: 王之涣
content:
白日依山尽
黄河入海流
欲穷千里目
更上一层楼
translation:
The white sun sets behind the mountains
The Yellow River flows into the sea
To see a thousand miles further
Climb up one more floor
annotation: Wang Zhihuan's most famous poem is basically ancient China's motivational poster. But it's earned that status — the imagery is breathtaking, and the philosophy is timeless. The higher you go, the more you see. Simple as that.

title: 咏鹅
author: 骆宾王
content:
鹅鹅鹅
曲项向天歌
白毛浮绿水
红掌拨清波
translation:
Goose, goose, goose
Curving their necks, they sing to the sky
White feathers floating on green water
Red paddles stirring clear waves
annotation: Legend says Luo Binwang wrote this at age seven. It's a children's classic that works because it's so vivid — you can see the white feathers, the green water, the red feet paddling. A seven-year-old captured what poets spend lifetimes chasing.

title: 悯农
author: 李绅
content:
锄禾日当午
汗滴禾下土
谁知盘中餐
粒粒皆辛苦
translation:
Hoeing grain under the midday sun
Sweat drips onto the soil beneath the plants
Who knows that every grain in the bowl
Comes from bitter toil
annotation: Every Chinese child learns this poem. It's a reminder that food doesn't just appear on the table — someone sweated for it. Li Shen's poem has survived over a thousand years because its message never gets old.

title: 望庐山瀑布
titleEn: Gazing at the Waterfall on Mount Lu
author: 李白
content:
日照香炉生紫烟
遥看瀑布挂前川
飞流直下三千尺
疑是银河落九天
translation:
Sunlight on Incense Burner Peak births purple mist
From afar, the waterfall hangs before the river
A torrent plunges straight down three thousand feet
As if the Milky Way were falling from the ninth heaven
annotation: Li Bai at his most dramatic and magnificent. The waterfall becomes a river falling from the stars themselves. This is romantic poetry at its peak — nature so grand it seems supernatural.

title: 早发白帝城
titleEn: Departing Early from White Emperor City
author: 李白
content:
朝辞白帝彩云间
千里江陵一日还
两岸猿声啼不住
轻舟已过万重山
translation:
At dawn I leave White Emperor City among colorful clouds
A thousand miles to Jiangling, returned in one day
On both banks, the cries of monkeys never cease
My light boat has already passed ten thousand mountains
annotation: Li Bai wrote this after being pardoned from exile. You can feel the joy and relief — the boat flying downriver, the mountains blurring past, the monkey calls fading behind him. Freedom has never sounded so fast.

title: 赠汪伦
author: 李白
content:
李白乘舟将欲行
忽闻岸上踏歌声
桃花潭水深千尺
不及汪伦送我情
translation:
Li Bai boards his boat, about to depart
Suddenly from the shore comes the sound of singing footsteps
The Peach Blossom Pool is a thousand feet deep
But not as deep as Wang Lun's farewell to me
annotation: A friendship poem that's become the gold standard for measuring how much someone cares. Li Bai says: the water is deep, but your friendship is deeper. Simple, heartfelt, unforgettable.

title: 黄鹤楼送孟浩然之广陵
titlePinyin: Huang He Lou Song Meng Haoran Zhi Guang Ling
titleEn: Seeing Meng Haoran Off at Yellow Crane Tower
author: 李白
content:
故人西辞黄鹤楼
烟花三月下扬州
孤帆远影碧空尽
唯见长江天际流
translation:
My old friend bids farewell at Yellow Crane Tower
In the misty flowers of March, heading down to Yangzhou
The lone sail's distant shadow vanishes in the blue sky
All I see is the Long River flowing to the edge of heaven
annotation: Li Bai watches his best friend sail away until the boat disappears. What remains is just the river, flowing on forever. It's a poem about letting go — and about how love persists even after someone vanishes from sight.

title: 望天门山
author: 李白
content:
天门中断楚江开
碧水东流至此回
两岸青山相对出
孤帆一片日边来
translation:
Heaven's Gate splits open as the Chu River breaks through
Green waters eastward flowing, here they turn back
Green mountains on both banks rise facing each other
A lone sail comes from the edge of the sun
annotation: Li Bai turns a mountain landscape into something almost cinematic — the river forcing through the gate, the mountains rising, the sail appearing from the sun. Nature as epic spectacle.

title: 独坐敬亭山
author: 李白
content:
众鸟高飞尽
孤云独去闲
相看两不厌
只有敬亭山
translation:
All the birds have flown away high
The lone cloud drifts off, carefree
We gaze at each other, never tiring
Only Jingting Mountain and I
annotation: After a lifetime of rejection and exile, Li Bai finds the only companion that never leaves: a mountain. There's loneliness here, but also peace. Sometimes solitude is a gift.

title: 夜宿山寺
titleEn: Staying Overnight at a Mountain Temple
author: 李白
content:
危楼高百尺
手可摘星辰
不敢高声语
恐惊天上人
translation:
The tower rises a hundred feet, perilous
Reach out your hand and pluck the stars
I dare not speak in a loud voice
For fear of startling the people in heaven
annotation: Li Bai's imagination turns a tall temple into a skyscraper touching the heavens. The joke is wonderful — he's so close to the stars that he has to whisper. Playful, magical, and very Li Bai.

title: 春夜喜雨
titleEn: Happy Rain on a Spring Night
author: 杜甫
content:
好雨知时节
当春乃发生
随风潜入夜
润物细无声
translation:
Good rain knows its season
It falls when spring arrives
Following the wind, it steals into the night
Nourishing all things, softly, without a sound
annotation: Du Fu celebrates rain the way most people celebrate sunshine. He sees it as a quiet act of kindness — nature nurturing the world while everyone sleeps. The phrase 'softly, without a sound' became a metaphor for gentle influence.

title: 春望
author: 杜甫
content:
国破山河在
城春草木深
感时花溅泪
恨别鸟惊心
烽火连三月
家书抵万金
白头搔更短
浑欲不胜簪
translation:
The nation is shattered, but mountains and rivers remain
In the city's spring, grass and trees grow deep
Moved by the times, flowers draw tears
Hating separation, birds startle the heart
Beacon fires have lasted three months
A letter from home is worth ten thousand gold
My white hair, scratched, grows ever shorter
Soon it won't hold a hairpin anymore
annotation: Written during the An Lushan Rebellion, this is Du Fu at his most heartbroken. Spring should be beautiful, but war has ruined everything. Even flowers make him cry. The image of him scratching his thinning hair is devastating — grief literally wearing him down.

title: 绝句
author: 杜甫
content:
两个黄鹂鸣翠柳
一行白鹭上青天
窗含西岭千秋雪
门泊东吴万里船
translation:
Two golden orioles sing in the green willow
A line of white egrets ascends the blue sky
My window frames the western hills' eternal snow
My door moors a boat from distant Wu, ten thousand miles away
annotation: Four perfect images, like four paintings hung together. Birds, sky, snow, boat — each line is a complete picture. Du Fu finds peace in this moment, but the 'ten thousand miles' hints at how far he is from home.

title: 登高
author: 杜甫
content:
风急天高猿啸哀
渚清沙白鸟飞回
无边落木萧萧下
不尽长江滚滚来
万里悲秋常作客
百年多病独登台
艰难苦恨繁霜鬓
潦倒新停浊酒杯
translation:
Wind fierce, sky high, the monkeys' cries are mournful
Islets clear, sand white, birds circle endlessly
Boundless falling leaves rustle as they descend
The endless Long River rolls on and on
Ten thousand miles from home, a perpetual autumn guest
A hundred years of illness, climbing alone
Hardship and regret frost my hair in abundance
Down and out, I've just put down my cup of cloudy wine
annotation: Often called the greatest poem in Chinese literature. Du Fu wrote it old, sick, and far from home. Every image — the wind, the monkeys, the falling leaves, the endless river — mirrors his inner desolation. And he can't even drink to forget. It's devastating.

title: 茅屋为秋风所破歌
titlePinyin: Mao Wu Wei Qiu Feng Suo Po Ge
titleEn: Song of My Cottage Unbroken by Autumn Winds
author: 杜甫
content:
八月秋高风怒号
卷我屋上三重茅
茅飞渡江洒江郊
高者挂罥长林梢
下者飘转沉塘坳
南村群童欺我老无力
忍能对面为盗贼
公然抱茅入竹去
唇焦口燥呼不得
归来倚杖自叹息
俄顷风定云墨色
秋天漠漠向昏黑
布衾多年冷似铁
娇儿恶卧踏里裂
床头屋漏无干处
雨脚如麻未断绝
自经丧乱少睡眠
长夜沾湿何由彻
安得广厦千万间
大庇天下寒士俱欢颜
风雨不动安如山
呜呼
何时眼前突兀见此屋
吾庐独破受冻死亦足
translation:
In eighth month, autumn winds howl in fury
They roll up three layers of thatch from my roof
The thatch flies across the river, scattered on the banks
High strands hang caught on tall treetops
Low ones swirl down into muddy ponds
Children from the south village bully this old man
They steal right before my eyes
Openly carrying my thatch into the bamboo
I shout until my lips are dry, but to no avail
I return, leaning on my staff, sighing
Suddenly the wind stops, clouds turn ink-black
Autumn sky darkens toward dusk
My cotton quilt for years has been cold as iron
My child kicks in sleep, tearing the lining
The roof leaks everywhere, not a single dry spot
Rain falls like hemp threads, never ceasing
Since the rebellion, I've barely slept
This long wet night — when will it end
If only I could have a mansion with a thousand rooms
To shelter all the world's poor scholars with smiling faces
Steady as a mountain, unmoved by wind and rain
Alas
When will such a house appear before my eyes
Even if my own cottage alone breaks and I freeze to death — enough!
annotation: Du Fu's roof is literally blown off by the wind, and his first thought isn't for himself — it's for everyone suffering. 'If only I could shelter all the poor scholars of the world.' This is why Du Fu is called the Poet Sage. His compassion has no ceiling.

title: 闻官军收河南河北
titlePinyin: Wen Guan Jun Shou He Nan He Bei
titleEn: Hearing the Government Army Recovers Henan and Hebei
author: 杜甫
content:
剑外忽传收蓟北
初闻涕泪满衣裳
却看妻子愁何在
漫卷诗书喜欲狂
白日放歌须纵酒
青春作伴好还乡
即从巴峡穿巫峡
便下襄阳向洛阳
translation:
Beyond the sword pass, word suddenly comes: Ji North is recovered
At first hearing, tears soak my robes
I look at my wife — where is her worry now?
I roll up my books, wild with joy
In daylight I'll sing and drink deeply
With spring as my companion, I'll go home
Straight from Ba Gorge through Wu Gorge
Then down to Xiangyang, on to Luoyang
annotation: This is Du Fu's happiest poem. After years of war, the lost lands are recovered. He's so excited he can barely contain himself — tears, singing, drinking, and already planning the trip home. The speed of the poem mirrors his racing heart.

title: 旅夜书怀
author: 杜甫
content:
细草微风岸
危樯独夜舟
星垂平野阔
月涌大江流
名岂文章著
官应老病休
飘飘何所似
天地一沙鸥
translation:
Fine grass, gentle breeze on the shore
A tall-masted boat alone in the night
Stars hang low over the vast plain
The moon surges as the great river flows
Fame — how could it come from writing?
An official should retire with age and illness
Drifting, what do I resemble?
A single sandpiper between heaven and earth
annotation: Du Fu on a boat at night, feeling impossibly small. The stars and river are enormous; he's just one tiny bird in the vastness. It's beautiful and lonely at the same time — the kind of poem that makes you feel both insignificant and deeply alive.

title: 月夜
author: 杜甫
content:
今夜鄜州月
闺中只独看
遥怜小儿女
未解忆长安
香雾云鬟湿
清辉玉臂寒
何时倚虚幌
双照泪痕干
translation:
Tonight in Fuzhou, the moon
She watches alone in her chamber
I pity my little children
Too young to remember Chang'an
Fragrant mist dampens her cloud-like hair
Cold moonlight chills her jade-white arms
When shall we lean together by the thin curtain
Both reflected, our tear stains dried?
annotation: Du Fu thinks of his wife watching the same moon, far away. He imagines her hair damp from the night mist, her arms cold in the moonlight. The most painful part? His children are too young to even miss him. Love across distance, written with surgical tenderness.

title: 山居秋暝
author: 王维
content:
空山新雨后
天气晚来秋
明月松间照
清泉石上流
竹喧归浣女
莲动下渔舟
随意春芳歇
王孙自可留
translation:
Empty mountains after fresh rain
The air turns autumn as evening falls
Bright moonlight shines between the pines
Clear springs flow over stones
Bamboo rustles — washerwomen returning
Lotus stirs — a fishing boat descends
Let the spring flowers fade as they may
A prince may linger here, content
annotation: Wang Wei was a painter as well as a poet, and this poem is basically a landscape painting in words. Moonlight, pine trees, flowing water, rustling bamboo — every sense is engaged. He's saying: forget the busy world. This is enough.

title: 送元二使安西
titlePinyin: Song Yuan Er Shi An Xi
titleEn: Seeing Yuan Er Off to Anxi
author: 王维
content:
渭城朝雨浥轻尘
客舍青青柳色新
劝君更尽一杯酒
西出阳关无故人
translation:
Morning rain in Wei City dampens the light dust
The guesthouse is green, the willow colors fresh
I urge you to finish one more cup of wine
West of Yang Pass, there are no old friends
annotation: One of the most famous farewell poems in Chinese. That last line says everything — once you leave, there's no one who knows you. So drink up while you can, while you're still among friends. It's been set to music for over a thousand years.

title: 相思
author: 王维
content:
红豆生南国
春来发几枝
愿君多采撷
此物最相思
translation:
Red beans grow in the southern land
In spring, how many branches bloom?
I hope you will gather them often
These things most evoke longing
annotation: Red beans as love tokens — Wang Wei turns a simple plant into a symbol of yearning. The poem is short but the feeling is enormous. Sometimes the smallest things carry the heaviest emotions.

title: 鹿柴
author: 王维
content:
空山不见人
但闻人语响
返景入深林
复照青苔上
translation:
In the empty mountains, no one to be heard
Yet echoes of human voices drift
Returning sunlight enters the deep forest
And shines again upon the green moss
annotation: Silence and sound, light and shadow — Wang Wei captures a moment that feels almost supernatural. The empty mountain isn't really empty. The light comes back. There's a whole philosophy of presence and absence in just twenty words.

title: 竹里馆
author: 王维
content:
独坐幽篁里
弹琴复长啸
深林人不知
明月来相照
translation:
Sitting alone in the deep bamboo
I play my qin and whistle long
In the deep woods, no one knows I'm here
Only the bright moon comes to shine on me
annotation: Wang Wei's ideal evening: alone in the bamboo, playing music, whistling, with only the moon as his audience. No pretense, no performance — just a man and nature in perfect harmony. This is what contentment looks like.

title: 九月九日忆山东兄弟
titlePinyin: Jiu Yue Jiu Ri Yi Shan Dong Xiong Di
titleEn: Remembering My Brothers on the Double Ninth
author: 王维
content:
独在异乡为异客
每逢佳节倍思亲
遥知兄弟登高处
遍插茱萸少一人
translation:
Alone in a foreign land, a stranger
Every festival, I miss my family twice as much
I know my brothers are climbing the heights
Planting dogwood, but one person is missing
annotation: Wang Wei wrote this at seventeen, already far from home on a festival day. The genius is in the last line — he doesn't say 'I miss them,' he says they're missing him. That shift in perspective makes it heartbreaking.

title: 使至塞上
author: 王维
content:
单车欲问边
属国过居延
征蓬出汉塞
归雁入胡天
大漠孤烟直
长河落日圆
萧关逢候骑
都护在燕然
translation:
With a single cart, I head toward the frontier
Passing Juyan, a vassal state
A tumbleweed flies beyond the Han border
Returning geese enter the Hu sky
In the vast desert, a lone smoke rises straight
Over the long river, the setting sun is round
At Xiao Pass I meet a scouting rider
The protector-general is at Mount Yanran
annotation: 'A lone smoke rises straight, the setting sun is round' — these two lines are considered the most perfect description of the frontier ever written. Wang Wei was also a painter, and you can see it. The geometry of the scene is flawless.

title: 鸟鸣涧
author: 王维
content:
人闲桂花落
夜静春山空
月出惊山鸟
时鸣春涧中
translation:
At ease, the cassia flowers fall
The night is still, the spring mountain empty
The rising moon startles the mountain birds
They cry out now and then in the spring stream
annotation: Wang Wei uses noise to show silence. The birds singing make the mountain feel even quieter. It's like the old trick of 'the clock ticking in an empty room.' The more you notice the small sounds, the more you feel the vast stillness around them.

title: 终南别业
author: 王维
content:
中岁颇好道
晚家南山陲
兴来每独往
胜事空自知
行到水穷处
坐看云起时
偶然值林叟
谈笑无还期
translation:
In middle age I grew fond of the Way
In my later years I settled at South Mountain's edge
When inspiration comes, I go alone
These joys are mine alone to know
I walk until the water ends
And sit watching the clouds rise
By chance I meet an old man in the woods
We laugh and talk, forgetting when to return
annotation: Wang Wei's philosophy of life distilled into eight lines. Walk until there's no path, then sit and watch the clouds. Don't force anything. The best moments happen when you stop planning them. This is Zen poetry at its finest.

title: 送别
author: 王维
content:
山中相送罢
日暮掩柴扉
春草明年绿
王孙归不归
translation:
In the mountains, I've seen you off
At dusk I close my brushwood gate
Next year when spring grass turns green
Will you, my friend, return or not?
annotation: After the farewell, the real loneliness begins. Wang Wei closes the gate and looks at the grass that will grow green again — but will his friend be there to see it? The question hangs in the air, unanswered.

title: 长恨歌
author: 白居易
content:
汉皇重色思倾国
御宇多年求不得
杨家有女初长成
养在深闺人未识
天生丽质难自弃
一朝选在君王侧
回眸一笑百媚生
六宫粉黛无颜色
春寒赐浴华清池
温泉水滑洗凝脂
侍儿扶起娇无力
始是新承恩泽时
云鬓花颜金步摇
芙蓉帐暖度春宵
春宵苦短日高起
从此君王不早朝
承欢侍宴无闲暇
春从春游夜专夜
后宫佳丽三千人
三千宠爱在一身
金屋妆成娇侍夜
玉楼宴罢醉和春
姊妹弟兄皆列土
可怜光彩生门户
遂令天下父母心
不重生男重生女
骊宫高处入青云
仙乐风飘处处闻
缓歌慢舞凝丝竹
尽日君王看不足
渔阳鼙鼓动地来
惊破霓裳羽衣曲
九重城阙烟尘生
千乘万骑西南行
翠华摇摇行复止
西出都门百余里
六军不发无奈何
宛转蛾眉马前死
花钿委地无人收
翠翘金雀玉搔头
君王掩面救不得
回看血泪相流散
黄埃散漫风萧索
云栈萦纡登剑阁
峨嵋山下少人行
旌旗无光日色薄
蜀江水碧蜀山青
圣主朝朝暮暮情
行宫见月伤心色
夜雨闻铃肠断声
天旋地转回龙驭
到此踌躇不能去
马嵬坡下泥土中
不见玉颜空死处
君臣相顾尽沾衣
东望都门信马归
归来池苑皆依旧
太液芙蓉未央柳
芙蓉如面柳如眉
对此如何不泪垂
春风桃李花开日
秋雨梧桐叶落时
南内北里多秋草
落叶满阶红不扫
梨园弟子白发新
椒房阿监青娥老
夕殿萤飞思悄然
孤灯挑尽未成眠
迟迟钟鼓初长夜
耿耿星河欲曙天
鸳鸯瓦冷霜华重
翡翠衾寒谁与共
悠悠生死别经年
魂魄不曾来入梦
临邛道士鸿都客
能以精诚致魂魄
为感君王辗转思
遂教方士殷勤觅
排空驭气奔如电
升天入地求之遍
上穷碧落下黄泉
两处茫茫皆不见
忽闻海上有仙山
山在虚无缥缈间
楼阁玲珑五云起
其中绰约多仙子
中有一人字太真
雪肤花貌参差是
金阙西厢叩玉扃
转教小玉报双成
闻道汉家天子使
九华帐里梦魂惊
揽衣推枕起徘徊
珠箔银屏迤逦开
云鬓半偏新睡觉
花冠不整下堂来
风吹仙袂飘飖举
犹似霓裳羽衣舞
玉容寂寞泪阑干
梨花一枝春带雨
含情凝睇谢君王
一别音容两渺茫
昭阳殿里恩爱绝
蓬莱宫中日月长
回头下望人寰处
不见长安见尘雾
惟将旧物表深情
钿合金钗寄将去
钗留一股合一扇
钗擘黄金合分钿
但教心似金钿坚
天上人间会相见
临别殷勤重寄词
词中有誓两心知
七月七日长生殿
夜半无人私语时
在天愿作比翼鸟
在地愿为连理枝
天长地久有时尽
此恨绵绵无绝期
translation:
The Han emperor, obsessed with beauty that could topple kingdoms
Sought for years but could not find
In the Yang family, a daughter had just grown up
Raised in seclusion, unknown to the world
Her natural beauty could not be hidden
One morning she was chosen to stand by the emperor's side
She turned her head and smiled — a hundred charms bloomed
The painted ladies of six palaces lost all their color
In spring's chill, she bathed at Huaqing Pool
Warm spring waters smooth on cream-like skin
Maidservants lifted her, too delicate to stand
This was the first time she received his favor
Cloud-like hair, flower face, golden step-swaying
In the lotus tent's warmth they passed the spring night
Spring nights are short — the sun rose high
From then on, the emperor held no more morning courts
Attending feasts, no leisure at all
Spring outings by day, his nights devoted to her alone
Three thousand beauties in the inner palace
All three thousand favors on one person alone
In golden chambers she dressed to serve at night
In jade towers, feasts ended, drunk with spring
Her brothers and sisters all received lands
How pitiful — her family's gate shone with glory
This made parents throughout the land
Value daughters over sons
The Li Palace high, entering blue clouds
Celestial music drifting everywhere
Slow songs and dances, silk and bamboo
All day the emperor could never watch enough
But war drums from Yuyang shook the earth
Breaking the Rainbow and Feather Garment Dance
Dust and smoke rose above the ninefold gates
A thousand carriages, ten thousand riders, heading southwest
The imperial banner swayed, advanced, then stopped
A hundred miles west of the capital gate
The six armies would not advance — nothing could be done
Her delicate eyebrows, turned, died before the horse
Her jeweled hairpiece fell to the ground, no one gathered it
Kingfisher pins, golden sparrows, jade hairpins
The emperor covered his face — he could not save her
Looking back, blood and tears flowed together
Yellow dust scattered, the wind desolate
Cloud-linked planks wound up to Sword Gate
Below Mount Emei, few travelers
Banners colorless, the sun's light thin
Shu's river green, Shu's mountains blue
The sovereign's heart, morning and evening, full of feeling
At the way station, the moon was a heartbreaking color
Night rain, the sound of bells — gut-wrenching
Heaven and earth turned, the dragon carriage returned
Here he lingered, unable to leave
Below Mawei Slope, in the earth
Her jade face was nowhere to be seen
Ministers and emperor looked at each other, robes wet with tears
Eastward toward the capital, he let the horse find its way
Returning, the pools and gardens were all as before
Taiye's lotus, Weiyang's willows
The lotus like her face, the willows like her eyebrows
Facing this, how could tears not fall
When spring wind opened peach and plum blossoms
When autumn rain fell on phoenix tree leaves
The southern and northern palaces, full of autumn grass
Fallen leaves covered the steps, red, unswept
Pear Garden musicians, hair newly white
Pepper Chamber attendants, young faces aged
Evening palace, fireflies flying, thoughts silent
The lone lamp burned down — still no sleep
Slowly the bells and drums marked the long night
The Milky River brightened toward dawn
Mandarin duck tiles, cold, frost heavy
The kingfisher quilt — cold, who to share it?
Long years since life-and-death parting
Her soul never came to enter his dreams
A Daoist from Linqiong, guest of Hongdu
Who could summon souls with sincere devotion
Moved by the emperor's restless longing
He sent the sorcerer to search earnestly
Riding the wind, driving the qi, swift as lightning
Ascending heaven, entering earth, searching everywhere
Up to the green void, down to the yellow springs
Both places vast and empty — nothing to be found
Suddenly, word of a fairy mountain at sea
A mountain in the mists, between being and non-being
Pavilions and towers, exquisite, rising from five-colored clouds
Among them, graceful, many fairy maidens
One among them named Taizhen
Snow skin, flower face — it might be her
At the golden tower's western chamber, she knocked the jade door
Sent Xiao Yu to report to Shuangcheng
Hearing the Han emperor's envoy had come
Her dreaming soul startled in the nine-flower curtain
She gathered her clothes, pushed up her pillow, rose and paced
Pearl screens and silver barriers opened in succession
Her cloud hair half-tilted, just woken from sleep
Her flower crown not yet straight, she descended the hall
Wind blew her fairy sleeves, floating, lifting
Still like the Rainbow and Feather Garment Dance
Her jade face lonely, tears streaming
Like a branch of pear blossoms in spring rain
With deep emotion, she gazed and thanked the emperor
Since parting, voice and face, both distant and dim
In Zhaoyang Palace, love and favor ended
In Penglai Palace, days and years were long
Looking back down at the human world
Could not see Chang'an, only dust and mist
She sent old tokens to show her deep feeling
A jeweled box and golden hairpin to be delivered
She kept one half of the hairpin, one half of the box
Broke the golden hairpin, divided the jeweled pieces
But let our hearts be as firm as gold and jade
In heaven or on earth, we will meet again
At parting, earnestly, she sent a message
In the words, a vow only the two hearts knew
On the seventh night of the seventh month, in the Hall of Eternal Life
At midnight, when no one was near, they whispered
In heaven, we wish to be birds flying side by side
On earth, we wish to be branches intertwined
Heaven and earth last long, but even they have an end
This sorrow — endless, without limit
annotation: Bai Juyi's masterpiece — a love story that's also a political tragedy. An emperor's obsession destroys his empire and kills the woman he loves. The poem is over 800 words, but it never drags. Every line pulls you deeper. The ending — 'this sorrow without end' — has become the Chinese phrase for eternal regret.

title: 琵琶行
author: 白居易
content:
浔阳江头夜送客
枫叶荻花秋瑟瑟
主人下马客在船
举酒欲饮无管弦
醉不成欢惨将别
别时茫茫江浸月
忽闻水上琵琶声
主人忘归客不发
寻声暗问弹者谁
琵琶声停欲语迟
移船相近邀相见
添酒回灯重开宴
千呼万唤始出来
犹抱琵琶半遮面
转轴拨弦三两声
未成曲调先有情
弦弦掩抑声声思
似诉平生不得志
低眉信手续续弹
说尽心中无限事
轻拢慢捻抹复挑
初为霓裳后六幺
大弦嘈嘈如急雨
小弦切切如私语
嘈嘈切切错杂弹
大珠小珠落玉盘
间关莺语花底滑
幽咽泉流冰下难
冰泉冷涩弦凝绝
凝绝不通声暂歇
别有幽愁暗恨生
此时无声胜有声
银瓶乍破水浆迸
铁骑突出刀枪鸣
曲终收拨当心画
四弦一声如裂帛
东船西舫悄无言
唯见江心秋月白
沉吟放拨插弦中
整顿衣裳起敛容
自言本是京城女
家在虾蟆陵下住
十三学得琵琶成
名属教坊第一部
曲罢曾教善才服
妆成每被秋娘妒
五陵年少争缠头
一曲红绡不知数
钿头银篦击节碎
血色罗裙翻酒污
今年欢笑复明年
秋月春风等闲度
弟走从军阿姨死
暮去朝来颜色故
门前冷落鞍马稀
老大嫁作商人妇
商人重利轻别离
前月浮梁买茶去
去来江口守空船
绕船月明江水寒
夜深忽梦少年事
梦啼妆泪红阑干
我闻琵琶已叹息
又闻此语重唧唧
同是天涯沦落人
相逢何必曾相识
我从去年辞帝京
谪居卧病浔阳城
浔阳地僻无音乐
终岁不闻丝竹声
住近湓江地低湿
黄芦苦竹绕宅生
其间旦暮闻何物
杜鹃啼血猿哀鸣
春江花朝秋月夜
往往取酒还独倾
岂无山歌与村笛
呕哑嘲哳难为听
今夜闻君琵琶语
如听仙乐耳暂明
莫辞更坐弹一曲
为君翻作琵琶行
感我此言良久立
却坐促弦弦转急
凄凄不似向前声
满座重闻皆掩泣
座中泣下谁最多
江州司马青衫湿
translation:
At the head of the Xunyang River, seeing off a guest at night
Maple leaves and reed flowers, autumn rustling
The host dismounts, the guest is in the boat
We raise cups to drink but have no music
Drunk but joyless, about to part sadly
At parting, the vast river soaks the moon
Suddenly, pipa music from the water
The host forgets to leave, the guest delays
Following the sound, we ask in the dark: who plays?
The pipa stops, she hesitates to speak
We move our boat closer, invite her to meet
Add lamps, relight, reopen the banquet
After a thousand calls, she finally emerges
Still holding the pipa, half hiding her face
She turns the pegs, plucks a few strings
Before the tune forms, feeling is already there
Each string suppressed, each note full of thought
As if telling a life of unfulfilled ambition
Brows lowered, fingers moving freely
Playing out endless things in her heart
Light gathering, slow twisting, stroking, picking
First the Rainbow Skirt, then the Liuyao
The thick strings clatter like sudden rain
The thin strings whisper like private talk
Clattering and whispering, mixed together
Like large and small pearls falling on a jade plate
Smooth as orioles singing beneath flowers
Gurgling like a spring flowing under ice
The icy spring cold and stiff, the strings seem to freeze
Frozen through, the sound briefly stops
A hidden sorrow, a secret regret is born
At this moment, silence surpasses sound
Like a silver bottle breaking, water bursting
Like cavalry charging, swords and spears clanging
At the end, she draws the plectrum across the heart
All four strings sound like tearing silk
East boat and west barge, silent
Only the autumn moon's white reflection in the river's center
She sets down the plectrum, inserts it among the strings
Straightens her clothes, rises with a composed face
She says: I was originally from the capital
My home was below Hama Ridge
At thirteen I learned the pipa and mastered it
My name was in the first rank of the Music Academy
When I finished playing, even the masters admired
When I dressed up, I was envied by Qiu Niang
Young men of the Five Tombs competed to give gifts
One song brought countless red silks
Jeweled hairpins beat time until they broke
Blood-red silk skirts were stained with spilled wine
This year's laughter, next year's again
Autumn moons and spring winds, idly passed
My brother left for the army, my aunt died
Evening came, morning went, my beauty faded
Before my gate, fewer and fewer horses
Grown old, I married a merchant's wife
The merchant values profit, lightly parts from me
Last month he went to Fuliang to buy tea
He leaves, I guard the empty boat at the river mouth
The bright moon circles the boat, the river water cold
Deep in night, I suddenly dream of young days
Dreaming tears, makeup streaked red
I heard the pipa and already sighed
Hearing her words, I sigh again
We are both lost souls at the edge of the world
Meeting — why must we have known each other before?
Since last year I left the imperial capital
Exiled, lying ill in Xunyang city
Xunyang is remote, no music
All year long, no sound of silk and bamboo
Living near the Pen River, low and damp
Yellow reeds and bitter bamboo surround my dwelling
What do I hear from dawn to dusk
Cuckoos weeping blood, monkeys crying sadly
Spring river, flower mornings, autumn moon nights
Often I pour wine and drink alone
Are there no mountain songs or village flutes?
Harsh and grating, hard to bear hearing
Tonight, hearing your pipa's voice
Like hearing celestial music, my ears briefly clear
Don't refuse — sit and play one more song
I'll compose a Pipa Song for you
Moved by my words, she stood long
Then sat back down, tightened the strings, urgent
Sad, not like the sound before
Everyone present heard and covered their face, weeping
Among those present, who shed the most tears?
The Jiangzhou Sima — his blue robe was soaked
annotation: Bai Juyi meets a pipa player who was once the most celebrated musician in the capital, now old and abandoned. He sees himself in her — both talented, both exiled, both forgotten. 'We are both lost souls at the edge of the world' is one of the most quoted lines in Chinese poetry. The music descriptions are legendary — you can almost hear the strings.

title: 赋得古原草送别
titlePinyin: Fu De Gu Yuan Cao Song Bie
author: 白居易
content:
离离原上草
一岁一枯荣
野火烧不尽
春风吹又生
远芳侵古道
晴翠接荒城
又送王孙去
萋萋满别情
translation:
Lush, lush, the grass on the plain
Each year it withers and blooms again
Wildfire cannot burn it all
Spring wind blows, and it grows again
Fragrant grass spreads over the ancient road
Bright green meets the ruined city
Once more I see a noble friend away
The thick grass is full of parting feeling
annotation: Bai Juyi wrote this at sixteen, and it made his reputation. The grass is a metaphor for resilience — no matter how many times life burns you down, you grow back. It's also a farewell poem, so the grass becomes a symbol of feelings that never die.

title: 暮江吟
author: 白居易
content:
一道残阳铺水中
半江瑟瑟半江红
可怜九月初三夜
露似真珠月似弓
translation:
A streak of setting sun spreads across the water
Half the river shimmers jade-green, half glows red
How lovely — the night of the third day of the ninth month
Dewdrops like pearls, the moon like a bow
annotation: Bai Juyi captures a sunset so perfectly you can see the colors. Half the river green, half red — nature's own painting. Then the night scene: pearl dewdrops and a crescent moon. It's a postcard from a thousand years ago.

title: 钱塘湖春行
author: 白居易
content:
孤山寺北贾亭西
水面初平云脚低
几处早莺争暖树
谁家新燕啄春泥
乱花渐欲迷人眼
浅草才能没马蹄
最爱湖东行不足
绿杨阴里白沙堤
translation:
North of Gushan Temple, west of Jia Pavilion
The water's surface just level, cloud feet low
Here and there, early orioles fight for warm trees
Whose house? New swallows peck at spring mud
Wild flowers gradually begin to dazzle the eye
Shallow grass just enough to cover a horse's hoof
What I love most — east of the lake, never enough walking
In the green willows' shade, the white sand embankment
annotation: Bai Juyi strolls around West Lake in spring, and everything is alive. Birds fighting for trees, swallows building nests, flowers blooming, grass growing. It's a joyful walk through nature waking up. You can feel his happiness in every line.

title: 卖炭翁
author: 白居易
content:
卖炭翁
伐薪烧炭南山中
满面尘灰烟火色
两鬓苍苍十指黑
卖炭得钱何所营
身上衣裳口中食
可怜身上衣正单
心忧炭贱愿天寒
夜来城外一尺雪
晓驾炭车辗冰辙
牛困人饥日已高
市南门外泥中歇
翩翩两骑来是谁
黄衣使者白衫儿
手把文书口称敕
回车叱牛牵向北
一车炭
千余斤
宫使驱将惜不得
半匹红纱一丈绫
系向牛头充炭直
translation:
A charcoal seller
Cutting wood and burning charcoal in the Southern Mountains
His face covered in dust and smoke
His temples gray, his ten fingers black
What does the charcoal money buy?
Clothes for his body, food for his mouth
Pitiful — his clothes are thin
But he worries the charcoal price is low and wishes for cold weather
Last night, a foot of snow outside the walls
At dawn he drives his charcoal cart over frozen ruts
The cow is tired, the man is hungry, the sun is high
He rests in the mud outside the south gate of the market
Two riders approach, graceful — who are they?
Yellow-robed messengers, white-clad boys
Holding documents, mouths declaring an imperial order
They turn the cart, shout at the ox, pull it north
One cart of charcoal
Over a thousand pounds
The palace envoy takes it — he cannot refuse
Half a bolt of red silk, a length of damask
Tied to the ox's head — this is the charcoal's price
annotation: Bai Juyi's social conscience on full display. An old man freezes all winter making charcoal, and the palace takes it for almost nothing. No commentary needed — the facts speak for themselves. This is poetry as protest, and it's devastating.

title: 长相思
author: 白居易
content:
汴水流
泗水流
流到瓜洲古渡头
吴山点点愁
思悠悠
恨悠悠
恨到归时方始休
月明人倚楼
translation:
The Bian River flows
The Si River flows
Flowing to the ancient ferry at Guazhou
The Wu mountains, dot by dot, full of sorrow
Thoughts stretch on and on
Resentment stretches on and on
Resentment only ends when he returns
In the bright moon, a person leans on the tower
annotation: Water flows endlessly, and so does longing. Bai Juyi uses the rivers as a metaphor for feelings that never stop. The final image — someone leaning on a tower in the moonlight, waiting — is one of the most iconic scenes in Chinese poetry.

title: 离思
author: 元稹
content:
曾经沧海难为水
除却巫山不是云
取次花丛懒回顾
半缘修道半缘君
translation:
Once having seen the vast sea, no other water compares
Apart from the clouds on Mount Wu, none are truly clouds
Passing through a thicket of flowers, I can't be bothered to look back
Half because of my spiritual path, half because of you
annotation: Yuan Zhen wrote this after his wife's death. The message: I've seen the greatest, and nothing else measures up. It's become the ultimate love poem in Chinese — a way of saying 'you were my everything, and I'll never love like that again.'

title: 行宫
author: 元稹
content:
寥落古行宫
宫花寂寞红
白头宫女在
闲坐说玄宗
translation:
Desolate, the ancient traveling palace
The palace flowers bloom red, in silence
White-haired palace women remain
Sitting idly, speaking of Xuanzong
annotation: Twenty words that tell an entire empire's story. The palace is empty, the flowers still bloom, and old women gossip about the good old days. The fall of a dynasty, told through a casual conversation. Yuan Zhen was a master of saying more with less.

title: 锦瑟
author: 李商隐
content:
锦瑟无端五十弦
一弦一柱思华年
庄生晓梦迷蝴蝶
望帝春心托杜鹃
沧海月明珠有泪
蓝田日暖玉生烟
此情可待成追忆
只是当时已惘然
translation:
The ornate zither, for no reason, has fifty strings
Each string, each bridge, recalls a flourishing year
Zhuangzi dreamed at dawn, lost among butterflies
The cuckoo carries the spring heart of the fallen king
In the sea's moonlight, pearls weep tears
At Lantian, warm sun, jade gives birth to mist
This feeling — could it have become a memory?
Even then, it was already lost and bewildered
annotation: Li Shangyin's most mysterious poem. Is it about love? Regret? Lost youth? Nobody knows for sure, and that's what makes it great. The images are gorgeous and strange — pearls crying, jade smoking, butterflies dreaming. It's a puzzle that rewards every rereading.

title: 夜雨寄北
author: 李商隐
content:
君问归期未有期
巴山夜雨涨秋池
何当共剪西窗烛
却话巴山夜雨时
translation:
You ask when I'll return — I don't know
Night rain in the Ba mountains swells the autumn pool
When shall we trim the candle by the western window together
And speak of this night rain in the Ba mountains?
annotation: Li Shangyin writes to his wife from a rainy mountain town. He can't tell her when he's coming home. But he imagines a future where they're together, talking about this very moment. The rain becomes a memory they'll share. It's heartbreaking and beautiful at the same time.

title: 无题
author: 李商隐
content:
相见时难别亦难
东风无力百花残
春蚕到死丝方尽
蜡炬成灰泪始干
晓镜但愁云鬓改
夜吟应觉月光寒
蓬山此去无多路
青鸟殷勤为探看
translation:
Hard to meet, and hard to part
The east wind is weak, the hundred flowers wither
The spring silkworm spins silk until death
The candle burns to ash before its tears dry
At dawn, gazing in the mirror, fearing her cloud-like hair has changed
At night, chanting, she must feel the moonlight's cold
From here to Penglai Mountain is not far
The blue bird will diligently go to check on her
annotation: Li Shangyin's most famous love poem. 'The silkworm spins until death, the candle burns to ash' — these lines have become the ultimate expression of devotion. Love that doesn't end, even when everything else does. The mystery of who he was writing to only adds to the allure.

title: 无题·昨夜星辰昨夜风
titleEn: Untitled: Last Night's Stars, Last Night's Wind
author: 李商隐
content:
昨夜星辰昨夜风
画楼西畔桂堂东
身无彩凤双飞翼
心有灵犀一点通
隔座送钩春酒暖
分曹射覆蜡灯红
嗟余听鼓应官去
走马兰台类转蓬
translation:
Last night's stars, last night's wind
West of the painted tower, east of the cassia hall
My body has no colorful phoenix's paired wings
But our hearts share a single rhinoceros horn's connection
Across seats, passing hooks, the spring wine warm
Divided into teams, guessing games, the candle red
Alas — I heard the drum and must go to office
Riding to the Orchid Terrace, like a tumbling tumbleweed
annotation: A magical night at a party, a connection with someone across the room. 'Our hearts share a rhinoceros horn's connection' — the idea that two people can understand each other without words. Then duty calls, and he's swept away. The longing for what might have been.

title: 登乐游原
author: 李商隐
content:
向晚意不适
驱车登古原
夕阳无限好
只是近黄昏
translation:
Toward evening, feeling out of sorts
I drive my car to the ancient terrace
The setting sun is infinitely beautiful
It's just that dusk is near
annotation: Four lines that capture a universal truth: the most beautiful moments are the most fleeting. The sunset is gorgeous precisely because it's about to disappear. Li Shangyin could have been talking about love, youth, or life itself. That ambiguity is why it endures.

title: 嫦娥
author: 李商隐
content:
云母屏风烛影深
长河渐落晓星沉
嫦娥应悔偷灵药
碧海青天夜夜心
translation:
On the mica screen, candle shadows deep
The long river sinks, morning stars fade
Chang'e must regret stealing the elixir of life
Over blue sea and green sky, night after night, her heart
annotation: The moon goddess who gained immortality but lost everything else. Li Shangyin asks: was it worth it? Eternal life means eternal loneliness. The 'blue sea, green sky, night after night' is one of the most desolate images in Chinese poetry.

title: 泊秦淮
author: 杜牧
content:
烟笼寒水月笼沙
夜泊秦淮近酒家
商女不知亡国恨
隔江犹唱后庭花
translation:
Mist veils the cold water, moonlight veils the sand
At night I moor on the Qinhuai, near a wine house
The singing girls know nothing of a fallen kingdom's grief
Across the river, they still sing 'Flowers of the Back Court'
annotation: Dumu watches entertainers singing a song associated with a dynasty's collapse, while the current dynasty crumbles around them. The irony is sharp: history repeats itself, and nobody notices. It's a poem about willful ignorance that still resonates today.

title: 赤壁
author: 杜牧
content:
折戟沉沙铁未销
自将磨洗认前朝
东风不与周郎便
铜雀春深锁二乔
translation:
A broken halberd sinks in the sand, iron not yet rusted
I clean and polish it, recognizing a former dynasty
If the east wind had not helped Zhou Yu
The Bronze Sparrow Tower in deep spring would have locked away the two Qiaos
annotation: Dumu finds a rusted weapon at the famous Red Cliff battlefield and reimagines history. What if the wind hadn't blown? The entire outcome would have changed. It's a reminder that history often hinges on the smallest chance events.

title: 山行
author: 杜牧
content:
远上寒山石径斜
白云生处有人家
停车坐爱枫林晚
霜叶红于二月花
translation:
Far up the cold mountain, a stone path winds
Where white clouds rise, there are homes
I stop my cart, loving the maple forest at dusk
Frost-red leaves surpass February flowers
annotation: Dumu discovers that autumn is more beautiful than spring. The frost-red maple leaves outshine fresh flowers. Sometimes what's fading is more gorgeous than what's beginning. A lesson in finding beauty in unexpected places.

title: 清明
author: 杜牧
content:
清明时节雨纷纷
路上行人欲断魂
借问酒家何处有
牧童遥指杏花村
translation:
During Qingming, rain falls thick and fast
Travelers on the road are nearly broken-hearted
I ask: where can I find a wine shop?
A cowherd points to distant Apricot Blossom Village
annotation: The rainiest, saddest, most beautiful festival poem. Qingming is tomb-sweeping day — a time to remember the dead. The traveler is lost in grief and rain, and a child points him toward wine. Sometimes the smallest kindness means everything.

title: 江南春
author: 杜牧
content:
千里莺啼绿映红
水村山郭酒旗风
南朝四百八十寺
多少楼台烟雨中
translation:
A thousand miles of orioles singing, green mirroring red
Water villages, mountain towns, wine banners in the wind
The Southern Dynasties' four hundred and eighty temples
How many towers and terraces stand in the misty rain?
annotation: Dumu paints the entire south of China in four lines. Birds, colors, villages, temples, rain — it's a panoramic masterpiece. The last line is haunting: all those grand temples, now just shapes in the mist. Empires fade, but the rain keeps falling.

title: 秋夕
author: 杜牧
content:
银烛秋光冷画屏
轻罗小扇扑流萤
天阶夜色凉如水
卧看牵牛织女星
translation:
Silver candle, autumn light, cold painted screen
A light silk fan chases fireflies
The palace steps, night cool as water
Lying down, she watches the Cowherd and Weaver stars
annotation: A palace woman alone on an autumn night, chasing fireflies with her fan. She lies down and looks at the stars — the legendary lovers separated by the Milky Way. She's trapped in her own separation, watching others' from afar. Quietly devastating.

title: 寄扬州韩绰判官
titlePinyin: Ji Yang Zhou Han Chuo Pan Guan
titleEn: To Judge Han Chuo in Yangzhou
author: 杜牧
content:
青山隐隐水迢迢
秋尽江南草未凋
二十四桥明月夜
玉人何处教吹箫
translation:
Blue mountains faint, waters stretching far
Autumn ends, but south of the river, grass hasn't withered
On the Twenty-Four Bridge, in the bright moon's night
Where is the jade beauty teaching someone to play the flute?
annotation: Dumu remembers Yangzhou's beauty and a friend who was there. The Twenty-Four Bridge in moonlight is one of the most romantic images in Chinese poetry. 'Where is she now?' — the question carries all the weight of time and distance.

title: 出塞
author: 王昌龄
content:
秦时明月汉时关
万里长征人未还
但使龙城飞将在
不教胡马度阴山
translation:
The moon of Qin, the pass of Han
Ten thousand miles of expedition — men have not returned
If only the Flying General of Dragon City were here
He would never let the barbarian horses cross Mount Yin
annotation: Wang Changling's frontier poem is a cry for peace through strength. If only we had the right general, the wars would end. It's been quoted by military leaders and politicians for over a millennium. The moon and pass have watched centuries of soldiers march out and not come back.

title: 芙蓉楼送辛渐
titlePinyin: Fu Rong Lou Song Xin Jian
titleEn: Seeing Xin Jian Off at Furong Tower
author: 王昌龄
content:
寒雨连江夜入吴
平明送客楚山孤
洛阳亲友如相问
一片冰心在玉壶
translation:
Cold rain joins the river, night enters Wu
At dawn I see you off, the Chu mountains alone
If my friends in Luoyang ask about me
Tell them: a heart of ice in a jade pot
annotation: Wang Changling was accused of wrongdoing, and this poem is his defense. 'A heart of ice in a jade pot' — pure, transparent, uncorrupted. It's become the ultimate expression of maintaining integrity when the world misunderstands you.

title: 从军行
author: 王昌龄
content:
青海长云暗雪山
孤城遥望玉门关
黄沙百战穿金甲
不破楼兰终不还
translation:
Over Qinghai, long clouds darken the snow mountains
A lone city gazes toward Jade Gate Pass
Yellow sand, a hundred battles pierce golden armor
We will not return until Loulan is destroyed
annotation: The frontier soldiers' oath: we won't come home until the enemy is defeated. The golden armor is sandpierced, the city is isolated, but their resolve is iron. Wang Changling captures the brutal glory of frontier warfare.

title: 闺怨
author: 王昌龄
content:
闺中少妇不知愁
春日凝妆上翠楼
忽见陌头杨柳色
悔教夫婿觅封侯
translation:
A young wife in her boudoir knows no sorrow
On a spring day, dressed in finery, she climbs the tower
Suddenly she sees the roadside willow's green
And regrets urging her husband to seek a noble title
annotation: One moment changes everything. She was happy, even proud that her husband went to seek glory. Then she sees the spring willows and realizes: what good is a title if you're alone? The green of spring makes her see the emptiness of her life.

title: 凉州词
author: 王翰
content:
葡萄美酒夜光杯
欲饮琵琶马上催
醉卧沙场君莫笑
古来征战几人回
translation:
Grape wine in a luminous cup
About to drink, the pipa urges us from horseback
Drunk, I lie on the battlefield — don't laugh
Since ancient times, how many who went to war returned?
annotation: A soldier's last drink before battle. 'Don't laugh if you see me drunk on the field — most soldiers don't come back anyway.' It's dark humor masking deep tragedy. The wine is beautiful, the music is urgent, and death is waiting.

title: 凉州词
author: 王之涣
content:
黄河远上白云间
一片孤城万仞山
羌笛何须怨杨柳
春风不度玉门关
translation:
The Yellow River rises far among white clouds
A lone city among ten-thousand-ren mountains
Why must the Qiang flute lament the willows?
The spring wind never crosses Jade Gate Pass
annotation: The frontier is so remote that even spring can't reach it. The Qiang flute plays sad songs about willows (a symbol of parting), but there's no spring here to be sad about. It's a poem about being beyond the edge of the world.

title: 回乡偶书
author: 贺知章
content:
少小离家老大回
乡音无改鬓毛衰
儿童相见不相识
笑问客从何处来
translation:
Left home young, return old
My accent unchanged, but my sideburns have thinned
Children see me but don't recognize me
Laughing, they ask: where does this guest come from?
annotation: He Zhizhang left home at thirty and returned at eighty. He's a stranger in his own hometown. The children's innocent question — 'where are you from?' — is the most painful thing he could hear. Home is the one place you can't return to.

title: 咏柳
author: 贺知章
content:
碧玉妆成一树高
万条垂下绿丝绦
不知细叶谁裁出
二月春风似剪刀
translation:
Jade-green dressed, a tree so tall
Ten thousand strands hang like green silk ribbons
Who carved these fine leaves so delicate?
The February spring wind — like a pair of scissors
annotation: He Zhizhang turns a willow tree into a work of art and credits spring wind as the sculptor. The image of wind as scissors cutting leaves is playful and original. It's a children's classic that adults never tire of.

title: 登幽州台歌
titleEn: Song of Climbing the Youzhou Terrace
author: 陈子昂
content:
前不见古人
后不见来者
念天地之悠悠
独怆然而涕下
translation:
Before me, no ancient ones
After me, no ones to come
Thinking of the vastness of heaven and earth
Alone, I weep
annotation: Chen Zi'ang stands on a terrace and feels the weight of all time. Behind him, the great figures of history. Ahead, nothing but the future. He's utterly alone in the immensity of existence. It's the most existential poem in Chinese literature — and it's only twenty-two words.

title: 白雪歌送武判官归京
titlePinyin: Bai Xue Ge Song Wu Pan Guan Gui Jing
titleEn: Song of White Snow: Seeing Judge Wu Back to the Capital
author: 岑参
content:
北风卷地白草折
胡天八月即飞雪
忽如一夜春风来
千树万树梨花开
散入珠帘湿罗幕
狐裘不暖锦衾薄
将军角弓不得控
都护铁衣冷难着
瀚海阑干百丈冰
愁云惨淡万里凝
中军置酒饮归客
胡琴琵琶与羌笛
纷纷暮雪下辕门
风掣红旗冻不翻
轮台东门送君去
去时雪满天山路
山回路转不见君
雪上空留马行处
translation:
The north wind rolls up the white grass, snapping it
In the barbarian sky, August brings sudden snow
As if a night of spring wind had blown
A thousand trees, ten thousand trees bloom with pear flowers
Scattering into pearl curtains, soaking silk curtains
Fox fur is not warm, silk quilt is thin
The general's horn bow cannot be controlled
The protector's iron armor is cold and hard to wear
The vast sea is frozen a hundred feet deep
Gloomy clouds condense for ten thousand miles
In the central army, wine is set for the returning guest
Huqin, pipa, and Qiang flute
Fluttering evening snow falls below the camp gate
The wind tugs the frozen red flag, it cannot turn
At the east gate of Luntai, we see you off
When you leave, snow fills the mountain road
The mountain road turns, turns, and you are no longer seen
Only the horse's tracks remain in the snow
annotation: Cen Shen's masterpiece of frontier poetry. The famous line 'As if a night of spring wind had blown, a thousand trees bloom with pear flowers' turns the desolate snowscape into a vision of spring — one of the most celebrated metaphors in Chinese poetry.

title: 过香积寺
author: 王维
content:
不知香积寺
数里入云峰
古木无人径
深山何处钟
泉声咽危石
日色冷青松
薄暮空潭曲
安禅制毒龙
translation:
I didn't know Xiangji Temple
Several miles into the cloud peaks
Ancient trees, no human path
Deep mountains — where does the bell come from?
Spring sound chokes on dangerous rocks
Sunlight chills the green pines
At dusk, by the empty pool's curve
Meditation tames the poisonous dragon
annotation: Wang Wei gets lost looking for a temple. The deeper he goes, the more mysterious it becomes. The 'poisonous dragon' is desire itself, tamed by stillness.

title: 汉江临眺
author: 王维
content:
楚塞三湘接
荆门九派通
江流天地外
山色有无中
郡邑浮前浦
波澜动远空
襄阳好风日
留醉与山翁
translation:
Chu's border meets the Three Xiang
Jingmen connects the nine streams
The river flows beyond heaven and earth
Mountain colors between being and nothing
Prefectures float on the distant shore
Waves move the far sky
Xiangyang has such fine weather
I'll stay drunk with the mountain old man
annotation: 'The river flows beyond heaven and earth, mountain colors between being and nothing.' Wang Wei paints with philosophy — the landscape dissolves into the infinite.

title: 观猎
author: 王维
content:
风劲角弓鸣
将军猎渭城
草枯鹰眼疾
雪尽马蹄轻
忽过新丰市
还归细柳营
回看射雕处
千里暮云平
translation:
Wind strong, horn bows sing
The general hunts at Wei City
Grass withered, eagle eyes sharp
Snow gone, horse hooves light
Suddenly past Xinfeng market
Back to the Willow Camp
Look back where the eagle was shot
Ten thousand miles of evening clouds, flat
annotation: Wang Wei watches a general hunt. Every line is action — wind, bows, eagles, horses. The final image: looking back at the hunt, everything is calm again under endless clouds.

title: 赋得古原草送别
titlePinyin: Fu De Gu Yuan Cao Song Bie
author: 白居易
content:
离离原上草
一岁一枯荣
野火烧不尽
春风吹又生
远芳侵古道
晴翠接荒城
又送王孙去
萋萋满别情
translation:
Lush, lush grass on the plain
Each year it withers and blooms again
Wildfire cannot burn it all
Spring wind blows and it grows again
Distant fragrance invades the old road
Bright green meets the ruined city
Once more I see a nobleman off
Thick grass, full of parting feelings
annotation: Bai Juyi was only 16 when he wrote this! The grass is a metaphor for resilience — no matter how many times life burns you down, you grow back.

title: 池上
author: 白居易
content:
小娃撑小艇
偷采白莲回
不解藏踪迹
浮萍一道开
translation:
A little child poles a little boat
Steals white lotus and returns
Not knowing how to hide his trail
The duckweed parts in one line
annotation: A child sneaks out to pick lotus flowers, leaving a trail of parted duckweed behind him. Childhood innocence in just 20 characters.

title: 问刘十九
author: 白居易
content:
绿蚁新醅酒
红泥小火炉
晚来天欲雪
能饮一杯无
translation:
Green ants on newly brewed wine
A small red clay fire stove
Evening comes, the sky wants to snow
Can you drink a cup with me?
annotation: The most inviting poem ever written. A warm fire, fresh wine, snow falling outside — and a simple question: will you join me?

title: 暮江吟
author: 白居易
content:
一道残阳铺水中
半江瑟瑟半江红
可怜九月初三夜
露似真珠月似弓
translation:
A streak of setting sun spreads on the water
Half the river jade-green, half red
How lovely, the night of the third of September
Dewdrops like pearls, the moon like a bow
annotation: Bai Juyi watches sunset paint the river in two colors. Then night falls — pearl-like dew and a crescent moon.

title: 大林寺桃花
author: 白居易
content:
人间四月芳菲尽
山寺桃花始盛开
长恨春归无觅处
不知转入此中来
translation:
In the world, April's flowers have all faded
The mountain temple's peach blossoms just begin to bloom
I always grieve that spring leaves with no place to find it
I didn't know it had turned and come here
annotation: Spring is gone from the valley — but up in the mountain temple, it's just beginning. Sometimes what you're looking for hasn't disappeared, it's just somewhere unexpected.

title: 嫦娥
author: 李商隐
content:
云母屏风烛影深
长河渐落晓星沉
嫦娥应悔偷灵药
碧海青天夜夜心
translation:
On the mica screen, candle shadows deepen
The Milky Way gradually sets, morning stars sink
Chang'e must regret stealing the elixir of life
Blue sea, green sky — night after night, her heart
annotation: The moon goddess has immortality but no one to love. Is eternal life worth eternal loneliness?

title: 贾生
author: 李商隐
content:
宣室求贤访逐臣
贾生才调更无伦
可怜夜半虚前席
不问苍生问鬼神
translation:
The Xuan Room seeks talent, visits the exiled
Jia Yi's ability surpasses all
Pitiable — at midnight, the seat is empty
He asks about ghosts and gods, not the people
annotation: The emperor summons a brilliant scholar at midnight — not to ask about governing, but about ghosts. Li Shangyin's sarcasm is sharp: rulers care more about the supernatural than their own people.

title: 隋宫
author: 李商隐
content:
乘兴南游不戒严
九重谁省谏书函
春风举国裁宫锦
半作障泥半作帆
translation:
In high spirits, he tours the south without caution
Who in the palace reads the warning letters?
In spring wind, the whole country cuts palace brocade
Half for mud guards, half for sails
annotation: The Sui emperor's extravagance: the whole country cuts silk just for his boats. Half for mud guards, half for sails. Luxury built on the people's exhaustion.

title: 泊秦淮
author: 杜牧
content:
烟笼寒水月笼沙
夜泊秦淮近酒家
商女不知亡国恨
隔江犹唱后庭花
translation:
Mist veils the cold water, moon veils the sand
At night I moor on the Qinhuai near a wine house
The singing girls don't know the grief of a fallen nation
Across the river, they still sing 'Flowers in the Back Court'
annotation: While the country crumbles, entertainers keep singing. Du Mu's anger is quiet but sharp — a warning that forgetting history dooms you to repeat it.

title: 寄扬州韩绰判官
titlePinyin: Ji Yang Zhou Han Chao Pan Guan
titleEn: To Judge Han Chuo in Yangzhou
author: 杜牧
content:
青山隐隐水迢迢
秋尽江南草未凋
二十四桥明月夜
玉人何处教吹箫
translation:
Green mountains faint, water stretches far
Autumn ends, but Jiangnan grass hasn't withered
On the Twenty-Four Bridge, a moonlit night
Where is the jade lady teaching flute playing?
annotation: Du Mu remembers Yangzhou's beauty — faint mountains, endless water, a moonlit bridge. He asks about a friend who taught music. It's nostalgia for a golden time.

title: 遣怀
author: 杜牧
content:
落魄江湖载酒行
楚腰纤细掌中轻
十年一觉扬州梦
赢得青楼薄幸名
translation:
Down and out, I wander the rivers and lakes with wine
Waists thin as Chu dancers, light as a swallow in the palm
Ten years — one dream of Yangzhou
All I won was a heartless reputation in the pleasure quarters
annotation: Du Mu looks back on his wild youth in Yangzhou. 'Ten years — one dream.' He's not proud, just wistful. The good times passed like a dream, and all that's left is a reputation.

title: 赠别
author: 杜牧
content:
娉娉袅袅十三余
豆蔻梢头二月初
春风十里扬州路
卷上珠帘总不如
translation:
Graceful, slender, just past thirteen
Like a cardamom bud at the tip of a branch in early spring
On the ten-mile road through Yangzhou in spring wind
Rolling up every pearl curtain — none compare to her
annotation: A 13-year-old girl, as delicate as a cardamom bud. Du Mu says: on the entire beautiful road through Yangzhou, she's the most beautiful thing. It's a love poem disguised as a farewell.

title: 金谷园
author: 杜牧
content:
繁华事散逐香尘
流水无情草自春
日暮东风怨啼鸟
落花犹似坠楼人
translation:
All the glorious things have scattered, chasing fragrant dust
The water flows without feeling, grass grows green on its own
At dusk, the east wind grieves, birds cry
Falling blossoms still resemble the woman who jumped from the tower
annotation: A wealthy man's garden, now ruined. A beautiful woman jumped from a tower for love. Now only falling blossoms remember her. Du Mu sees beauty and tragedy in every petal.

title: 出塞
author: 王昌龄
content:
秦时明月汉时关
万里长征人未还
但使龙城飞将在
不教胡马度阴山
translation:
The bright moon of Qin, the pass of Han
Ten thousand miles of march, none have returned
If only the Flying General of Dragon City were here
No barbarian horse would cross Yin Mountain
annotation: Wang Changling wishes for a great general to protect the border. It's a poem about war's endless cycle and the longing for peace.

title: 从军行
author: 王昌龄
content:
青海长云暗雪山
孤城遥望玉门关
黄沙百战穿金甲
不破楼兰终不还
translation:
Over Qinghai, long clouds darken the snow mountains
A lone city gazes toward Jade Gate Pass
Yellow sand, a hundred battles pierce golden armor
We won't return until Loulan is destroyed
annotation: Surrounded by enemies, far from home, armor worn through — but they won't give up. Wang Changling captures the soldier's oath: victory or nothing.

title: 芙蓉楼送辛渐
titlePinyin: Fu Rong Lou Song Xin Jian
titleEn: Seeing Off Xin Jian at Lotus Tower
author: 王昌龄
content:
寒雨连江夜入吴
平明送客楚山孤
洛阳亲友如相问
一片冰心在玉壶
translation:
Cold rain joins the river, night enters Wu
At dawn I see you off, Chu mountains alone
If friends in Luoyang ask about me
Tell them: an icy heart in a jade pot
annotation: Accused of wrongdoing, Wang Changling sends his friend with a message: my heart is as pure as ice in jade. One of the most famous declarations of innocence in Chinese poetry.

title: 凉州词
author: 王翰
content:
葡萄美酒夜光杯
欲饮琵琶马上催
醉卧沙场君莫笑
古来征战几人回
translation:
Grape wine in a luminous cup
About to drink, the pipa urges from horseback
Laugh not if I drunk-ly fall on the battlefield
Since ancient times, how many soldiers return?
annotation: Drink now, because tomorrow we may die. Wang Han doesn't glorify war — he shows soldiers choosing joy in the face of death. 'Laugh not if I fall drunk on the battlefield.'

title: 别董大
author: 高适
content:
千里黄云白日曛
北风吹雁雪纷纷
莫愁前路无知己
天下谁人不识君
translation:
A thousand miles of yellow clouds, the white sun dims
North wind blows, geese fly through falling snow
Don't worry about the road ahead lacking friends
Under heaven, who doesn't know you?
annotation: In the middle of a snowstorm, Gao Shi sends off a friend with the most encouraging words ever written: 'Don't worry — everyone knows you!' Sometimes that's all someone needs to hear.

title: 塞上听吹笛
titleEn: Hearing a Flute on the Frontier
author: 高适
content:
雪净胡天牧马还
月明羌笛戍楼间
借问梅花何处落
一夜关山尽望乡
translation:
Snow clears, barbarian sky, horses return home
Bright moon, Qiang flute between the watchtowers
I ask: where do the plum blossoms fall?
All night, every pass and mountain looks toward home
annotation: A flute plays a homesick melody on the frontier. The soldiers listen and think of home. 'Where do the plum blossoms fall?' — everywhere, and nowhere.

title: 逢雪宿芙蓉山主人
titlePinyin: Feng Xue Xue Fu Rong Shan Zhu Ren
titleEn: Seeking Shelter in Snow at Lotus Mountain
author: 刘长卿
content:
日暮苍山远
天寒白屋贫
柴门闻犬吠
风雪夜归人
translation:
At dusk, blue mountains far
Sky cold, white house poor
By the brush gate, dogs bark
In wind and snow, a night traveler returns
annotation: Four images, like four photographs: distant mountains, a poor house, barking dogs, a traveler in the snow. Liu Changqing tells a whole story in 20 characters.

title: 送灵澈上人
author: 刘长卿
content:
苍苍竹林寺
杳杳钟声晚
荷笠带斜阳
青山独归远
translation:
Dark, dark the bamboo forest temple
Faint, faint the evening bell
A straw hat carries the setting sun
Alone, he returns to the distant green mountains
annotation: A monk walks back to his temple at sunset. The bell fades, the sun sets, the mountains wait. Liu Changqing paints a monk's life as the most peaceful thing in the world.

title: 送崔九
author: 裴迪
content:
归山深浅去
须尽丘壑美
莫学武陵人
暂游桃源里
translation:
Return to the mountains, deep or shallow
You must exhaust all the valleys' beauty
Don't learn from the man of Wuling
Who only visited Peach Blossom Spring briefly
annotation: If you're going to retreat to the mountains, really retreat. Don't be a tourist in nature — commit to it. Pei Di's advice: go all the way.

title: 春泛若耶溪
titleEn: Spring Boat on the Ruoye Stream
author: 綦毋潜
content:
幽意无断绝
此去随所偶
晚风吹行舟
花路入溪口
际夜转西壑
隔山望南斗
潭烟飞溶溶
林月低向后
translation:
Secluded feelings, never ending
This time I'll go wherever I drift
Evening wind blows the boat
A road of flowers enters the stream mouth
Past night, turn west through ravines
Across mountains, gaze at the southern dipter
Pool mist flies, dissolving
Forest moon, low, behind
annotation: A boat drifts where it wants. Flowers line the banks, mist rises, the moon follows. This is what freedom feels like — no plan, no destination, just the stream.

title: 宿建德江
author: 孟浩然
content:
移舟泊烟渚
日暮客愁新
野旷天低树
江清月近人
translation:
I move my boat to a misty islet
At dusk, the traveler's sorrow grows new
Wilderness wide, sky lower than the trees
River clear, the moon close to people
annotation: Moored at dusk on a misty river. The sky seems to press down, but the moon feels close. Meng Haoran finds both loneliness and comfort in nature.

title: 宿桐庐江寄广陵旧游
titlePinyin: Su Tong Lu Jiang Ji Guang Ling Jiu You
titleEn: Mooring on the Tonglu River: To Old Friends in Guangling
author: 孟浩然
content:
山暝听猿愁
沧江急夜流
风鸣两岸叶
月照一孤舟
建德非吾土
维扬忆旧游
还将两行泪
遥寄海西头
translation:
Mountains dark, I hear grieving apes
The great river rushes in the night wind
Wind rustles leaves on both banks
Moon shines on one lonely boat
Jiande is not my homeland
I remember old friends in Yangzhou
I'll send these two lines of tears
Far to the west sea's edge
annotation: Far from home, listening to apes cry and rivers rush. The moon shines on his lonely boat. Meng Haoran sends his tears — literally — to friends far away.

title: 早寒有怀
author: 孟浩然
content:
木落雁南度
北风江上寒
我家襄水曲
遥隔楚云端
乡泪客中尽
孤帆天际看
迷津欲有问
平海夕漫漫
translation:
Trees shed leaves, geese fly south
North wind, the river grows cold
My home curves by the Xiang River
Far beyond Chu's clouds
Tears of homesickness, all spent in travel
A lone sail at the edge of the sky
Lost at the ford, I want to ask
The flat sea, evening stretches endlessly
annotation: Autumn cold, geese flying south, and the poet far from home. He's lost — literally at a river crossing, metaphorically in life. The endless evening sea mirrors his confusion.

title: 留别王维
author: 孟浩然
content:
寂寂竟何待
朝朝空自归
欲寻芳草去
惜与故人违
当路谁相假
知音世所稀
只应守寂寞
还掩故园扉
translation:
Silent, silent — what am I waiting for?
Morning after morning, I return empty-handed
I want to seek fragrant grass and go
But regret leaving my old friend
Who in power will help me?
True friends are rare in this world
I should just keep my loneliness
And close my garden gate
annotation: Failed in his career, Meng Haoran decides to go home. His friend Wang Wei is the only thing making him hesitate. 'True friends are rare' — so he'll go home and be alone with his garden.

title: 与诸子登岘山
titlePinyin: Yu Zhu Zi Deng Xian Shan
author: 孟浩然
content:
人事有代谢
往来成古今
江山留胜迹
我辈复登临
水落鱼梁浅
天寒梦泽深
羊公碑尚在
读罢泪沾襟
translation:
Human affairs have their rise and fall
Coming and going make the past and present
Rivers and mountains keep the great traces
Our generation climbs again
Water falls, the fish weir grows shallow
Sky cold, the Dream Marsh grows deep
Lord Yang's stele still stands
After reading it, tears soak my collar
annotation: Climbing a mountain where a great man once stood. The weir is shallow, the marsh is deep, but the monument remains. Meng Haohan weeps — not for the past, but for everyone who tried and was forgotten.

title: 渭川田家
author: 王维
content:
斜阳照墟落
穷巷牛羊归
野老念牧童
倚杖候荆扉
雉雊麦苗秀
蚕眠桑叶稀
田夫荷锄至
相见语依依
即此羡闲逸
怅然吟式微
translation:
Slanting sun shines on the village
Deep lanes, cattle and sheep return
An old countryman thinks of the shepherd boy
Leans on his stick, waits by the brush gate
Pheasants call, wheat sprouts flourish
Silkworms sleep, mulberry leaves thin
Farmers arrive with hoes
Meet and talk, reluctant to part
At this I envy their leisure
Melancholy, I sing 'Shi Wei'
annotation: Wang Wei watches farmers returning home at sunset. They're tired but content. He envies them — they have what he doesn't: a simple life with clear meaning.

title: 青溪
author: 王维
content:
言入黄花川
每逐青溪水
随山将万转
趣途无百里
声喧乱石中
色静深松里
漾漾泛菱荇
澄澄映葭苇
我心素已闲
清川澹如此
translation:
They say enter the Yellow Flower River
I always follow the green stream
Following the mountain, ten thousand turns
The journey is less than a hundred miles
Sound noisy among scattered rocks
Color still among deep pines
Rippling, floating water chestnuts
Clear, reflecting reeds
My heart has always been at leisure
The clear stream is this tranquil
annotation: A stream that twists ten thousand times but goes less than a miles as the crow flies. Wang Wei sees himself in it — busy on the outside, peaceful within.

title: 西施咏
author: 王维
content:
艳色天下重
西施宁久微
朝为越溪女
暮作吴宫君
贱日岂殊众
贵来方悟稀
邀人傅香粉
不自著衣
君宠益骄态
君怜无是非
translation:
Beauty is valued under heaven
How could Xi Shi stay obscure long?
Morning: a girl by the Yue stream
Evening: a lady in the Wu palace
In humble days, how was she different?
Only in honor did people realize her rarity
She asked others to apply her makeup
She didn't dress herself
The more the king favored her, the prouder she grew
The more he loved her, the less right or wrong mattered
annotation: Xi Shi went from washing silk to ruling a palace. But Wang Wei's real question is: when everyone treats you as special, can you still tell right from wrong?

title: 老将行
author: 王维
content:
少年十五二十时
步行夺得胡马射
射杀山中白额虎
肯数邺下黄须儿
一身转战三千里
一剑曾当百万师
汉兵奋迅如霹雳
虏骑崩腾畏蒺藜
translation:
When he was fifteen or twenty
Walking, he captured a barbarian horse
He shot and killed the white-browed tiger in the mountains
He wouldn't yield to the yellow-bearded hero of Ye
His single body fought across three thousand miles
His single sword once faced a million soldiers
Han troops charged like thunder
Barbarian cavalry crumbled, fearing caltrops
annotation: A young warrior's incredible feats — capturing horses, killing tigers, facing armies alone. But this is the beginning of a longer story: what happens when the hero grows old and forgotten?

title: 桃源行
author: 王维
content:
渔舟逐水爱山春
两岸桃花夹古津
坐看红树不知远
行尽青溪不见人
山口潜行始隈隈
山开旷望旋平陆
遥看一处攒云树
近入千家散花竹
translation:
A fishing boat follows the water, loving the mountain spring
Both banks of peach blossoms line the ancient ford
Sitting, watching red trees, not knowing the distance
Walking to the end of the green stream, seeing no one
At the mountain pass, creeping forward, winding
The mountain opens, a broad view, suddenly flat land
From afar, a place where clouds and trees gather
Up close, a thousand homes scattered with flowers and bamboo
annotation: Wang Wei retells the famous Peach Blossom Spring story. A fisherman discovers a hidden paradise. The journey is as beautiful as the destination — winding paths, sudden openings, clouds and flowers.

title: 和贾至舍人早朝大明宫之作
titlePinyin: He Jia Zhi She Ren Zao Chao Da Ming Gong Zhi Zuo
titleEn: Morning Audience at the Great Bright Palace: Matching Jia Zhi's Poem
author: 王维
content:
绛帻鸡人报晓筹
尚衣方进翠云裘
九天阊阖开宫殿
万国衣冠拜冕旒
日色才临仙掌动
香烟欲傍衮龙浮
朝罢须裁五色诏
佩声归到凤池头
translation:
Red-turbaned rooster man announces the dawn
The wardrobe official presents the green cloud robe
The nine heavens, the palace gates open
Ten thousand nations' robes and caps bow to the crown
Sunlight just touches, the immortal palm moves
Incense smoke wants to drift beside the dragon robe
After court, he must cut five-colored edicts
The sound of jade pendants returns to the Phoenix Pool
annotation: The imperial court in all its glory — dawn announcements, cloud robes, ten thousand nations bowing. Wang Wei captures the spectacle of power at sunrise.

title: 积雨辋川庄作
titlePinyin: Ji Yu Wang Chuan Zhuang Zuo
titleEn: After Persistent Rain at Wangchuan Villa
author: 王维
content:
积雨空林烟火迟
蒸藜炊黍饷东菑
漠漠水田飞白鹭
阴阴夏木啭黄鹂
山中习静观朝槿
松下清斋折露葵
野老与人争席罢
海鸥何事更相疑
translation:
Persistent rain, empty forest, smoke rises slowly
Steaming greens, cooking millet, feeding the eastern fields
Vast, vast water fields, white egrets fly
Shady, shady summer trees, yellow orioles sing
In the mountains, practicing stillness, watching morning mallows
Under pines, pure meals, picking dew-kissed mallows
The old countryman has stopped fighting for seats with people
Why should the seagulls still suspect him?
annotation: Rain, smoke, egrets, orioles — Wang Wei's mountain life is full of small beauties. The final line: he's so at peace that even the seagulls trust him. This is what enlightenment looks like.

title: 渔歌子
author: 张志和
content:
西塞山前白鹭飞
桃花流水鳜鱼肥
青箬笠
绿蓑衣
斜风细雨不须归
translation:
Before West Fort Mountain, white egrets fly
Peach blossoms on the water, mandarin fish fat
Green bamboo hat
Green straw cloak
Slanting wind, fine rain — no need to return
annotation: A fisherman in the rain who doesn't want to go home. The fish are fat, the blossoms are falling, the rain is gentle. Why would you leave a perfect moment?

title: 滁州西涧
author: 韦应物
content:
独怜幽草涧边生
上有黄鹂深树鸣
春潮带雨晚来急
野渡无人舟自横
translation:
I alone love the quiet grass growing by the ravine
Above, yellow orioles sing in deep trees
Spring tide with rain, evening comes urgent
At the wild crossing, no one — the boat lies sideways
annotation: A lonely boat at a deserted crossing. The grass is quiet, the birds sing, the rain rushes in. Wei Yingwu finds beauty in abandonment.

title: 秋夜寄邱员外
titlePinyin: Qiu Ye Ji Qiu Yuan Wai
author: 韦应物
content:
怀君属秋夜
散步咏凉天
空山松子落
幽人应未眠
translation:
I think of you on an autumn night
Walking, singing to the cool sky
In the empty mountain, pine nuts fall
The hermit must not be sleeping either
annotation: Walking on a cool autumn night, thinking of a friend. Pine nuts fall in the silence. Wei Yingwu imagines his friend is also awake, also thinking. Distance disappears in shared solitude.

title: 寒食
author: 韩翃
content:
春城无处不飞花
寒食东风御柳斜
日暮汉宫传蜡烛
轻烟散入五侯家
translation:
Spring city — nowhere without flying flowers
Cold Food Festival, east wind, palace willows slant
At dusk, the Han palace passes candles
Light smoke drifts into the five marquises' homes
annotation: During the Cold Food Festival, no fires are allowed — except in the palace. Candles are passed to the wealthy. Han Hong's subtle critique: even fire follows power.

title: 江村
author: 杜甫
content:
清江一曲抱村流
长夏江村事事幽
自去自来梁上燕
相亲相近水中鸥
老妻画纸为棋局
稚子敲针作钓钩
但有故人供禄米
微躯此外更何求
translation:
A clear river curves around the village
Long summer, the river village — everything serene
Coming and going freely, swallows on the beam
Close and intimate, gulls in the water
My old wife draws paper as a chess board
My young son hammers a needle into a fishing hook
If only old friends provide rice
What more could this small body ask for?
annotation: Du Fu's rare happy poem. His wife plays chess, his son makes fishing hooks, birds come and go. After years of war, he's found peace. 'What more could I ask for?' — everything.

title: 客至
author: 杜甫
content:
舍南舍北皆春水
但见群鸥日日来
花径不曾缘客扫
蓬门今始为君开
盘飧市远无兼味
樽酒家贫只旧醅
肯与邻翁相对饮
隔篱呼取尽余杯
translation:
South and north of my cottage, all spring water
I only see flocks of gulls coming daily
The flower path was never swept for guests
The brush gate now opens for you
The market is far, no variety of dishes
The wine jar is poor, only old brew
Would you drink with the old neighbor?
Across the fence, I'll call him to finish the cups
annotation: A friend visits Du Fu's humble cottage. No fancy food, just old wine and neighborly warmth. 'I'll call the old man over!' — this is friendship at its most genuine.

title: 江南逢李龟年
titlePinyin: Jiang Nan Feng Li Gui Nian
author: 杜甫
content:
岐王宅里寻常见
崔九堂前几度闻
正是江南好风景
落花时节又逢君
translation:
In Prince Qi's mansion, I often saw you
Before Cui Jiu's hall, I heard you many times
Now, in Jiangnan's beautiful scenery
In the season of falling flowers, I meet you again
annotation: Du Fu meets a famous musician from the old days. They were both young and the empire was glorious. Now they're old, the empire has fallen, and they meet among falling flowers. The whole poem is in what's not said.

title: 石壕吏
author: 杜甫
content:
暮投石壕村
有吏夜捉人
老翁逾墙走
老妇出门看
吏呼一何怒
妇啼一何苦
听妇前致词
三男邺城戍
一男附书至
二男新战死
存者且偷生
死者长已矣
室中更无人
惟有乳下孙
有孙母未去
出入无完裙
老妪力虽衰
请从吏夜归
急应河阳役
犹得备晨炊
translation:
At dusk I stop at Stone Moat Village
An official comes at night to seize men
The old man climbs over the wall and runs
The old woman goes out to see
The official shouts — how angry!
The woman weeps — how bitter!
I hear the woman speak:
Three sons guard the city of Ye
One son sent a letter
Two sons recently died in battle
The living barely survive
The dead are gone forever
In the house, no one left
Only a grandson at the breast
The grandson's mother hasn't left
But she has no complete skirt to wear
This old woman, though weak
Asks to follow the official tonight
To serve at Heyang
She can still prepare the morning meal
annotation: Du Fu witnesses a conscription officer taking an old woman whose three sons have already died in war. She volunteers to cook for the army. This is war's true face — not glory, but an old woman's sacrifice.

title: 新安吏
author: 杜甫
content:
客行新安道
喧呼闻点兵
借问新安吏
县小更无丁
府帖昨夜下
次选中男行
中男绝短小
何以守王城
肥男有母送
瘦男独伶俜
白水暮东流
青山犹哭声
translation:
A traveler walks the Xin'an road
Shouting, I hear the conscription
I ask the Xin'an official
The county is small, no adult men left
The order came last night
Next, draft the teenage boys
The boys are very small and short
How can they defend the royal city?
Fat boys have mothers seeing them off
Thin boys are alone and helpless
White water flows east at dusk
Green mountains still echo with crying
annotation: Du Fu watches teenage boys being drafted. They're too small to hold weapons. Some have mothers; some are alone. The mountains themselves seem to weep.

title: 无家别
author: 杜甫
content:
寂寞天宝后
园庐但蒿藜
我里百余家
世乱各东西
存者无消息
死者为尘泥
贱子因阵败
归来寻旧蹊
久行见空巷
日瘦气惨凄
但对狐与狸
竖毛怒我啼
四邻何所有
一二老寡妻
translation:
Lonely after Tianbao
My garden and hut are only weeds
My village had over a hundred families
The world fell apart, each went a different direction
The living have no news
The dust and mud
I, a lowly one, because the battle was lost
Returned to find my old path
Walking long, I see empty lanes
The sun thin, the air miserable
Only foxes and badgers
Bristle their fur and snarl at me
What remains of my neighbors?
One or two old widows
annotation: A soldier returns home to find his village destroyed. Only foxes live in the ruins. One or two old widows are all that remain. Du Fu shows war's aftermath — not victory, but emptiness.

title: 垂老别
author: 杜甫
content:
四郊未宁静
垂老不得安
子孙阵亡尽
焉用身独完
投杖出门去
同行为辛酸
幸有牙齿存
所悲骨髓干
男儿既介胄
长揖别上官
老妻卧路啼
岁暮衣裳单
translation:
The four suburbs are not peaceful
In old age, I cannot rest
My sons and grandsons have all died in battle
Why should I alone survive?
I throw down my stick and go out
My companions weep for me
At least I still have my teeth
But my bones are dry
A man in armor
Bows deeply to his superior
My old wife lies weeping on the road
At year's end, her clothes are thin
annotation: An old man's grandsons have all died in war. He picks up his stick and goes to fight. His wife lies weeping on the road. This is the most heartbreaking of Du Fu's war poems.

title: 梦李白
author: 杜甫
content:
死别已吞声
生别常恻恻
江南瘴疠地
逐客无消息
故人入我梦
明我长相忆
君今在罗网
何以有羽翼
恐非平生魂
路远不可测
魂来枫林青
魂返关山黑
落月满屋梁
犹疑照颜色
translation:
Dead separation has swallowed my voice
Living separation constantly grieves
The southland, land of miasma
The exile has no news
My old friend enters my dream
Knowing I always remember him
You are now in a net
How do you have wings?
I fear it's not your living soul
The road is far, immeasurable
Your soul comes through blue maple forests
Your soul returns through dark passes and mountains
The setting moon fills the roof beams
I still doubt I see your face
annotation: Du Fu dreams of his exiled friend Li Bai. He's not sure if it's really his soul or just his longing. The moon fills the room — is that really you? The uncertainty makes it more painful.

title: 喜见外弟又言别
titlePinyin: Xi Jian Wai Di You Yan Bie
titleEn: Happy to See My Cousin, Then Say Goodbye
author: 李益
content:
十年离乱后
长大一相逢
问姓惊初见
称名忆旧容
别来沧海事
语罢暮天钟
明日巴陵道
秋山又几重
translation:
After ten years of chaos and separation
We meet again, now grown
I ask your surname, surprised at first sight
When you say your name, I remember your old face
We talk of all that's happened since we parted
After talking, the evening sky bell rings
Tomorrow on the Baling road
Autumn mountains, layer upon layer
annotation: Two cousins meet after ten years of war. They don't recognize each other at first. They talk all night, and then — goodbye again. The mountains between them are both literal and metaphorical.

title: 夜上受降城闻笛
titlePinyin: Ye Shang Shou Xiang Cheng Wen Di
titleEn: Climbing the Surrender City at Night, Hearing a Flute
author: 李益
content:
回乐峰前沙似雪
受降城外月如霜
不知何处吹芦管
一夜征人尽望乡
translation:
Before Hui Le Peak, sand like snow
Outside Surrender City, moon like frost
Somewhere, a reed pipe plays
All night, every soldier looks toward home
annotation: Sand like snow, moon like frost — the frontier is beautiful and desolate. One flute, and every soldier thinks of home. Li Yi captures the universal homesickness of war.

title: 塞下曲
author: 卢纶
content:
林暗草惊风
将军夜引弓
平明寻白羽
没在石棱中
translation:
Forest dark, grass startled by wind
The general draws his bow at night
At dawn, he looks for the white feather
It's embedded in the stone's edge
annotation: A general shoots at what he thinks is a tiger in the dark. At dawn, he finds his arrow buried in a stone. This is the legend of Li Guang, the 'Flying General' — strength so great it defies physics.

title: 逢入京使
titleEn: Meeting a Messenger Going to the Capital
author: 岑参
content:
故园东望路漫漫
双袖龙钟泪不干
马上相逢无纸笔
凭君传语报平安
translation:
East toward home, the road stretches endlessly
Both sleeves wet with tears, never dry
We meet on horseback, no paper or pen
I ask you to carry word: I'm safe
annotation: Far from home on the frontier, Cen Shen meets a messenger. No paper, no pen — just 'tell them I'm safe.' The simplest message carries the deepest love.

title: 白雪歌送武判官归京
titlePinyin: Bai Xue Ge Song Wu Pan Guan Gui Jing
titleEn: Song of White Snow: Seeing Off Judge Wu to the Capital
author: 岑参
content:
北风卷地白草折
胡天八月即飞雪
忽如一夜春风来
千树万树梨花开
散入珠帘湿罗幕
狐裘不暖锦衾薄
将军角弓不得控
都护铁衣冷难着
瀚海阑干百丈冰
愁云惨淡万里凝
中军置酒饮归客
胡琴琵琶与羌笛
纷纷暮雪下辕门
风掣红旗冻不翻
轮台东门送君去
去时雪满天山路
山回路转不见君
雪上空留马行处
translation:
North wind rolls the ground, white grass snaps
Barbarian sky, August, and already snow flies
Suddenly, like one night of spring wind
Ten thousand trees, ten thousand trees of pear blossoms
Scattering into pearl curtains, dampening silk curtains
Fox fur not warm, brocade quilt thin
The general's horn bow cannot be controlled
The protector's iron armor, cold, hard to wear
The vast sea, a hundred zhang of ice
Gloomy clouds, ten thousand miles frozen
The central army sets wine for the returning guest
Hu qin, pipa, and Qiang flute
Thick evening snow falls by the camp gate
Wind tugs the red flag, frozen, it won't flutter
At Luntai's east gate, I see you off
When you leave, snow fills the mountain road
The mountain turns, the path turns — I can't see you
On the snow, only the traces of your horse
annotation: The most famous snow poem in Chinese. Cen Shen compares snow to pear blossoms — the most beautiful metaphor for the coldest thing. The final image: you're gone, only hoofprints remain in the snow.

title: 走马川行奉送封大夫出师西征
titlePinyin: Zou Ma Chuan Xing Song Song Feng Da Fu Chu Shi Xi Zheng
titleEn: Horse-Running River Song: Seeing Off General Feng's Western Campaign
author: 岑参
content:
君不见走马川行雪海边
平沙莽莽黄入天
轮台九月风夜吼
一川碎石大如斗
随风满地石乱走
匈奴草黄马正肥
金山西见烟尘飞
汉家大将西出师
将军金甲夜不脱
半夜军行戈相拨
风头如刀面如割
马毛带雪汗气蒸
五花连钱旋作冰
幕中草檄砚水凝
虏骑闻之应胆慑
料知短兵不敢接
车师西门伫献捷
translation:
Have you not seen Horse-Running River by the snow sea?
Flat sand, vast, yellow into the sky
Luntai, September, wind howls at night
A river of stones as big as bushels
Following the wind, stones scatter across the ground
The Xiongnu's grass turns yellow, horses grow fat
West of the Golden Mountain, dust and smoke fly
The Han general marches west
The general's golden armor, not removed at night
At midnight, the army marches, spears clashing
The wind's edge like a knife, the face like it's being cut
Horse fur carries snow, sweat steams
Five-spotted coins instantly freeze
In the tent, drafting orders, the ink water freezes
The barbarian cavalry should be terrified
They won't dare meet us in close combat
At Che Shi's west gate, we'll present the victory
